title: Height
short: Vertical distance above a chosen reference level, measured in metres (m).
full:
Height is the vertical position of an object measured from a reference level you are free to choose. It enters gravitational potential energy, E = m·g·h: only the change in height between start and finish affects the energy.

Because the reference is arbitrary, potential energy can be made zero at any convenient level — what is physical is always a difference of heights.

The SI unit is the metre (m).
